The lateral view on an x-ray provides critical information about vertebral alignment, which is essential to diagnose spondylolisthesis. In contrast, while other imaging modalities like MRI can show soft tissue involvement and detailed spinal cord anatomy, they do not provide as much direct evidence regarding the relative position of one vertebra compared to another. The AP (anterior-posterior) view also shows important bony details but lacks the side-to-side comparison needed for diagnosing slippage. Therefore,
